```{r} 
# Required packages

#install.packages("plyr")
#install.packages("ggplot2")
require(pryr)
require(ISLR)
require(boot)
library(data.table)
library(plyr)
library(ggplot2)
```
Step 1
```{r}
# Read file from computer via prompt
# Use sep="," to correctly parse the columns
Student <- read.table("C:/Users/cruos/Downloads/Assignment6.txt", header = TRUE, sep = ",")

View(Student)
# Run "mean" using Sex as the category via plyr
# StudentAverage = ddply(Student,"Sex",transform,Grade.Average=mean(Grade))
StudentAverage <- ddply(Student, "Sex", transform, Grade.Average = mean(Grade))

View(StudentAverage)
# Write output to file
write.table(StudentAverage, "Students_Gendered_Mean.txt", row.names = FALSE)

```
Step 2

```{r}
# Filter for names containing the letter 'i'
i_students <- subset(Student, grepl("i", Student$Name, ignore.case = TRUE))
View(i_students)
```
Step 3

```{r}
# Write filtered data set to CSV
write.csv(i_students, "Filtered_Names_I.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

Visual Representation

```{r}
summary_data <- ddply(Student, "Sex", summarise, Mean_Grade = mean(Grade))

ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = Sex, y = Mean_Grade, fill = Sex)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Mean_Grade, 2)), vjust = -0.5) + #vjust moves the labels above the bars for increased visibility
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Average Grade Performance by Sex",
       subtitle = "Calculated via plyr Split-Apply-Combine",
       y = "Mean Grade",
       x = "Category") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Female" = "#3498db", "Male" = "#e74c3c"))

```

Blog Link:
https://rstue.blogspot.com/2026/03/module-8-inputoutput-string.html
